frameworks.name: distributed working memory
frameworks.one_line: Working memory arises from interactions between prefrontal and sensory cortex representations

frameworks.name: high-dimensional integrative representations
frameworks.one_line: PFC encodes arbitrary combinations via high-dimensional mixed selectivity

frameworks.name: structured sensory representations
frameworks.one_line: Sensory cortex contributes feature-specific tuning to working memory content

frameworks.name: interference-limited capacity
frameworks.one_line: Capacity limit emerges from interference among co-active distributed representations

core_question: Do the flexibility and limited capacity of working memory reflect the same underlying neural mechanism?
key_assumptions: Working memory holds about three to four items at once
Flexibility requires representing arbitrary task-relevant content
Distributed coding across PFC and sensory cortex underlies maintenance
key_findings: Evidence converges on PFC-sensory interactions supporting flexible maintenance
Distributed representations cause interference between concurrently held items
Brain employs mechanisms that reduce interference and raise effective capacity
interpretations: Flexibility and limited capacity are two sides of the same distributed-coding mechanism
Effective capacity reflects a balance between representational flexibility and interitem interference
